---
title: "Olivia Work"
format: html
---

```{r}
#this chunk creates the inital bar chart
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

num_insurance <- read.csv("../data/raw/num_insurance.csv")

# Sum all states
us_totals <- num_insurance %>%
  summarise(across(-c(Location, Total), sum))

# Convert to long format
us_long <- us_totals %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Coverage",
    values_to = "People"
  )

# Calculate label positions and alternating vjust
us_long <- us_long %>%
  arrange(desc(Coverage)) %>%
  mutate(
    pos = cumsum(People) - People / 2,
    vjust_val = ifelse(row_number() %% 2 == 0, 4.5, -3.5)  # alternates below/above
  )

# Plot
ggplot(us_long, aes(x = "United States", y = People, fill = Coverage)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(y = pos, label = Coverage, vjust = vjust_val),
    color = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "U.S. Health Insurance Coverage Totals",
    x = "",
    y = "People"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    aspect.ratio = 0.15
  )

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(tibble)
library(janitor)

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40"),
      axis.title    = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
)
```

## Data Cleaning

```{r}
raw_coverage <- read_csv("../data/raw/Heathcare_Coverage_US.csv")

df <- raw_coverage %>%
  select(-Total) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Location), !str_starts(Location, "Notes")) %>%
  drop_na(Employer) %>%
  rename_with(~ c("location", "employer", "non_group", "medicaid",
                  "medicare", "military", "uninsured"), everything())

# Separate national from state-level (territories)
national <- df |> filter(location == "United States")
states   <- df |> filter(location != "United States")
```

### Correlation Between Coverage Types

```{r}
#| fig-height: 7

library(tidyverse)

# correlation matrix
corr_vals <- states %>%
  select(-location) %>%
  cor()

vars <- colnames(corr_vals)

cor_mat <- as.data.frame(corr_vals) %>%
  rownames_to_column("var1") %>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "corr") %>%
  mutate(
    row = match(var1, vars),
    col = match(var2, vars)
  ) %>%
  filter(row >= col) %>%   # lower triangle
  mutate(
    var1 = factor(var1, levels = vars),
    var2 = factor(var2, levels = rev(vars))   # flips y-axis nicely
  )

ggplot(cor_mat, aes(var1, var2, fill = corr)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(corr, 2)), size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#E74C3C",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Lower Triangle Correlation Matrix",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )
```


```{r}
library(plotly)
library(tidyverse)

cor_mat <- cor_mat %>%
  mutate(
    relation = case_when(
      corr > 0 ~ "increase",
      corr < 0 ~ "decrease",
      TRUE ~ "no change"
    ),
    
    tooltip = paste0(
      "An increase in <b>", var1,
      "</b> coverage is correlated with an <b>",
      relation,
      "</b> in <b>", var2,
      "</b> coverage",
      "<br><br>Correlation: ", round(corr, 2)
    )
  )

p <- ggplot(cor_mat, aes(x = var1, y = var2, fill = corr, text = tooltip)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  
  # keeps correlation numbers visible
  geom_text(aes(label = round(corr, 2)), size = 3.5) +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#E74C3C",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#2980B9",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1,1)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Interactive Correlation Matrix",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
```
